title: 8 Time-Tested Herbal Essential Oils for Beating Difficulty Chewing

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for difficulty chewing because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the mouth and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow food.
Shogaol is good for difficulty chewing because it helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth, making it easier to eat and chew food.
Zingiberene is good for difficulty chewing because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making it easier to eat and chew food.
Bisabolol is good for difficulty chewing because it helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth and gums, which can make chewing easier and less painful.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, can be helpful when you have difficulty chewing. This is because ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. When you have difficulty chewing, it's often due to pain or discomfort in these areas. The ginger oil can calm down the inflammation and ease the pain, making it easier to move your mouth and jaw. Additionally, ginger oil can also help to relax the muscles in your face and jaw, which can become tight and stiff when you're in pain. This relaxation can help to reduce the tension and discomfort that makes chewing difficult.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with difficulty chewing, you'll need fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves by placing them in a single layer on a paper towel and letting them air dry for several days. Once dry, chop the leaves into small pieces and transfer them to a clean glass jar. Next, fill a saucepan with water and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat and carefully place the jar with the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the water. Allow the leaves to steep in the hot water for about 30 minutes, then carefully remove the jar and let it cool. Strain the liquid into another container, discarding the solids, and let the liquid sit for a few hours to allow the liquid to settle. Finally, mix the liquid with a carrier oil in a ratio of about 1:5 (one part Ginkgo biloba liquid to five parts carrier oil) and store the mixture in a dark glass bottle. This Ginkgo biloba essential oil can be used topically, with a few drops applied to the affected area to help ease difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for difficulty chewing

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil, also known as licorice root oil, is a natural remedy that can help with difficulty chewing. This oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. When the jaw and mouth are inflamed, it can be hard to open the mouth or chew food, making it difficult to eat. By reducing inflammation, the oil can help to relax the jaw and mouth muscles, making it easier to chew and swallow food. Additionally, the oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections in the mouth and gums, further reducing discomfort and difficulty when eating. The oil can be used in aromatherapy, applied topically to the skin, or added to food to help soothe and calm the mouth and jaw.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with difficulty chewing, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. Start by purchasing the leaves from a reputable supplier or growing them yourself in a controlled environment. Next, dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent mold growth. You can do this by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel and letting them air dry for several days. Once the leaves are completely dry, crush them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, place the powdered leaves in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid. Next, add a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to the jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Finally, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the oil to infuse and extract the beneficial compounds from the Ginkgo biloba leaves. After the infusion process is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil is now ready to use, but remember to always dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin or using it in aromatherapy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oil is good for difficulty chewing because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. This makes it easier to move your mouth and jaw to chew food. The oil also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the tissue in your mouth and jaw from damage and repair it. Curcuma longa oil can help reduce inflammation and pain caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which can make chewing difficult. The oil's antibacterial properties can also help prevent infections in the mouth, which can make chewing and eating painful. By reducing pain and inflammation, Curcuma longa essential oil can make it easier to chew and eat, improving digestion and overall health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with difficulty chewing, you need fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. First, clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or moisture. Then, fill a clean glass jar with the dried leaves, making sure they are completely covered with the leaves. Next, pour a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil over the leaves, making sure the leaves are completely submerged. The general ratio is to use one part dried leaves to three parts carrier oil. After that, store the jar in a cool and dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the leaves infuse their properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it as needed, always diluting it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin or gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oil, also known as sage oil, is good for difficulty chewing because it helps soothe mouth and throat pain. This oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it reduces swelling and redness. When you can't chew easily, it may be because your mouth and throat are sore or inflamed. Sage oil's anti-inflammatory properties calm down these areas, making it easier to eat and swallow. Additionally, sage oil has antibacterial properties, which help kill germs that can cause mouth and throat infections. By using sage oil, you may find that your mouth and throat feel better, allowing you to chew and swallow more easily.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that help with difficulty chewing, you'll need a few basic items. First, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, and dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. Start by filling a small saucepan with water and bringing it to a boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer and add a handful of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the water. Let the leaves steep in the hot water for about 20-25 minutes, or until the water has reduced slightly and the leaves have released their active compounds. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids. Next, add a few drops of the cooled liquid to a small amount of carrier oil, stirring well to combine. This mixture will be your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil. Apply a few drops to your tongue or gums as needed to help with difficulty chewing, but be sure to do a patch test first to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint essential oil, has a long history of use for digestive issues. Its main benefit for difficulty chewing is due to its ability to relax the muscles in the mouth and throat. When you have trouble chewing, it can be painful and uncomfortable. Peppermint oil's cooling sensation can help ease this discomfort by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles. This allows the jaw and throat to move freely, making it easier to chew and swallow food. Additionally, peppermint oil's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can often worsen difficulty chewing. By reducing tension and promoting relaxation, peppermint oil can help make eating and chewing more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that may help with difficulty chewing, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. First, place a handful of dried leaves in a clean glass jar. Next, pour a neutral oil, such as grapeseed or sweet almond oil, over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Then, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the oil to extract the active compounds from the Ginkgo biloba leaves. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the oil, gently massage a few drops onto the affected area or inhale it through steam inhalation to help ease difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oil, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, has a beneficial effect on difficulty chewing due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When we experience difficulty chewing, it's often because of inflammation in the mouth and jaw area. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce this inflammation, making it easier to chew and swallow food. This can be especially helpful for people who experience pain or stiffness in the jaw and mouth, making eating and speaking uncomfortable. The oil's warmth and analgesic properties also help to ease pain and discomfort in the affected areas, allowing for smoother and more efficient chewing. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum essential oil has been known to improve circulation and reduce swelling, further contributing to its beneficial effects on difficulty chewing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil for difficulty chewing, start by drying and grinding 10-15 Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder. Next, mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 4 ounces of carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil turns a light greenish color and has a slightly nutty smell. Then, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the oil in a cool, dark place and use it within 6 months. To use the oil for difficulty chewing, apply a few drops to your tongue or gums 2-3 times a day, and massage it gently. The Ginkgo biloba essential oil may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oil is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. When we have difficulty chewing, it's usually because our jaw or mouth is swollen or sore. The anti-inflammatory properties in Echinacea purpurea oil can help reduce swelling and ease pain. This can make it easier to move our jaw and chew our food. Additionally, the oil may also help calm the digestive system, which can be upset when we have trouble eating or swallowing. Some people also believe that Echinacea purpurea oil can help boost the immune system, which can help fight off infections that might be causing our difficulty chewing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil for difficulty chewing, first, gather the necessary materials: dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), a double boiler, cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and a dark glass bottle. Next, combine 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in the double boiler, and heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil turns greenish in color and has a strong herbal scent. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly. Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, and discard the solids. Transfer the oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-5 drops of the Ginkgo biloba essential oil with a carrier oil for massage or apply it to the affected area to help ease difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is helpful for people who have trouble chewing. This is because it improves blood flow to the mouth and teeth. When blood flow increases, it brings more oxygen and nutrients to the area. This can help to heal gum disease and mouth sores, which can make eating and chewing painful. Ginkgo biloba oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and jaw. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba oil may help to make chewing easier and more comfortable. It can also help to reduce anxiety and stress related to eating, which can also contribute to difficulty chewing. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba oil may help to improve cognitive function, which can aid in proper chewing and swallowing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with difficulty chewing, start by harvesting fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves. You'll need about a pound of leaves for a small batch. Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent any contamination. Next, steam distill the leaves to extract the oil. This process involves placing the leaves in a distillation apparatus and heating them with steam, causing the oil to vaporize and rise into a condenser, where it cools and turns back into a liquid oil. Collect the oil and filter it to remove any impurities. Ginkgo biloba oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help with difficulty chewing by reducing swelling and pain in the jaw and mouth. However, it's essential to note that this oil should not be ingested and should only be used topically, after diluting it with a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil. Always consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist before using any herbal essential oil, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

intro_desc: Difficulty chewing, also known as dysphagia, can be a frustrating and painful experience. It can make eating and drinking a challenge, leading to weight loss, dehydration, and social isolation. However, there are natural remedies that can help alleviate this issue. Herbal essential oils, particularly those derived from 'Zingiber officinale' (Ginger) and 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (Licorice root), have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the muscles and tissues in the mouth and throat. Ginger, for example, has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain, making it easier to chew and swallow food. Licorice root, on the other hand, has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and mouth ulcers, reducing discomfort and pain.  Using herbal essential oils can help improve the quality of life for individuals with difficulty chewing. By reducing inflammation and pain, these oils can make eating and drinking a more enjoyable experience. This, in turn, can help prevent weight loss and dehydration, and even reduce the risk of social isolation. Furthermore, using natural remedies like herbal essential oils can be a more gentle and non-invasive approach to managing difficulty chewing, compared to medication or surgery. By incorporating these oils into your daily routine, you can take the first step towards regaining control over your eating and drinking habits, and improving your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: A combination of Ginger (Zingiber officinale), Turmeric (Curcuma longa), and Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) essential oils may help remedy difficulty chewing. Ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and mouth. Turmeric oil contains curcumin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth and jaw. Licorice root oil has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated tissues and reduce pain. This combination may help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with difficulty chewing, and promote a faster recovery.
supplementary_avoid: If you have difficulty chewing, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils. Peppermint oil (Mentha x piperita) and eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus) can be too strong and irritating for your mouth and gums. They may cause discomfort, pain, or even inflammation, making it even harder to chew. Similarly, clove oil (Syzygium aromaticum) and wintergreen oil (Gaultheria procumbens) contain compounds that can stimulate the nerves in your mouth, leading to increased sensitivity and pain when chewing. Additionally, tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) and oregano oil (Origanum vulgare) have antimicrobial properties, but they can also be too harsh for your mouth tissues, potentially causing further irritation. These oils are usually used in small amounts in aromatherapy, but if you have difficulty chewing, it's best to avoid using them altogether.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies can help with difficulty chewing. For example, warm compresses can be applied to the jaw and face to help relax the muscles and improve blood flow. This can be especially helpful for people who experience tension headaches or jaw pain while eating. Another option is to try massaging the jaw and face with a gentle, circular motion, which can help relax the muscles and improve flexibility. Some people also find that consuming warm, soothing foods and liquids, such as soup or broth, can be easier to chew than cold or hard foods. Additionally, trying to eat slowly and mindfully, taking small bites and chewing thoroughly, can also help to make eating more comfortable. Drinking plenty of water can also help to keep the mouth and jaw lubricated, making it easier to chew.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have difficulty chewing often experience other health issues. One common problem is digestive problems, such as bloating and constipation. This is because when food is not properly chewed, it can be hard for the body to digest. Medicinal herbs like ginger and peppermint can help ease digestive issues. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the digestive tract, while peppermint can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines.  Another issue people with chewing difficulties may face is dry mouth, a condition where the saliva in the mouth is reduced. This can make it harder for food to move through the digestive system and can lead to bad breath and tooth decay. Medicinal herbs like slippery elm and aloe vera can help soothe and moisturize the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort.  Some people may also experience jaw pain and tension due to straining while chewing. This can be caused by issues like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder or teeth grinding. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and licorice root can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to jaw tension. Licorice root can also help reduce inflammation in the jaw and surrounding muscles.  In addition, people with chewing difficulties may experience malnutrition, as they may not be able to consume enough food to meet their nutritional needs. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and cayenne pepper can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making it easier to absorb nutrients from food. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing. Cayenne pepper can help stimulate digestion and improve blood flow to the digestive system.  Finally, people with chewing difficulties may experience fatigue and low energy, as their body may not be able to absorb the nutrients it needs. Medicinal herbs like ginseng and reishi mushroom can help boost energy and reduce fatigue. Ginseng has adaptogenic properties that can help the body adapt to stress and promote energy, while reishi mushroom can help support immune function and reduce inflammation.
